title: 3Sum
description: Given an array of integers nums, return all the triplets [nums[i], nums[j], nums[k]] such that i != j, i != k, and j != k, and nums[i] + nums[j] + nums[k] == 0. Notice that the solution set must not contain duplicate triplets.

examples.explanation: The triplet [-1, -1, 2] sums to 0 and is valid. The triplet [-1, 0, 1] also sums to 0 and is valid. The triplet [-1, 1, 0] is a duplicate of [-1, 0, 1], so it is not included.
There are no triplets in the array that sum to zero; thus, the output is an empty list.
The triplet [-2, 0, 2] sums to 0 and is valid. The triplet [-2, 1, 1] also sums to 0 and is valid. No other combinations yield a valid triplet.

constraints: The length of nums is at most 3000.
Each element of nums is in the range [-10^5, 10^5].
hints: Consider sorting the array first to simplify finding triplets.
Use a two-pointer technique after fixing one number to find pairs that sum to a target.
Be careful with duplicate values to avoid counting the same triplet multiple times.
Think about how changing the fixed number affects the search space for the remaining two numbers.
